Section 1  
Define the function with input and output requirements.

Section 2  
Initialize a dictionary to store the most common improvement for each age group.

Section 3  
Iterate through each participant in the input list.

Section 4  
For each participant, update the frequency count of each improvement in their age group.

Section 5  
After processing all participants, determine the most common improvement for each age group.

Section 6  
Return the dictionary with the most common improvement for each age group.

* Use a dictionary to track the frequency of improvements per age group.  
* Select the improvement with the highest frequency for each age group.
